column4: Recall the somewhat distinct declension of this stem.
This is actually the past absolutive participle of the verb √car “act, do,” but often used substantively to mean the thing or things that someone has done.
Note that this verb is intransitive (unlike the English equivalents, which can be both transitive and intransitive).
Note that this first-class verb forms its present stem with reduplication. (Compare ἵστημι).
